description: Small, grainy skin growths caused by the human papillomavirus (HPV).
A common noncancerous skin growth that usually appears as a brown, black or light tan growth on the face, chest, shoulders or back.
symptoms: Waxy or scaly, slightly raised growth
Look like they were 'stuck on' the skin
lifestyle_tips: Don't try to pick or scrape them off

description: Common benign moles that are typically harmless but should be monitored for changes.
symptoms: Uniform tan, brown or black color
medical_solutions: Surgical excision if atypical or bothersome
lifestyle_tips: Monitor for ABCDE changes (Asymmetry, Border, Color, Diameter, Evolving)

description: A skin condition that causes small, fluid-filled blisters on the palms of the hands and the sides of the fingers.
lifestyle_tips: Use lukewarm water and gentle cleansers
Protect hands from moisture with gloves

description: A condition where hair follicles become plugged with oil and dead skin cells.
symptoms: Large, solid, painful lumps under the skin (nodules)
medical_solutions: Topical Benzoyl Peroxide to kill bacteria
Topical or oral antibiotics for inflammatory acne
natural_remedies: Tea tree oil for its antibacterial properties
Honey and cinnamon mask for inflammation
lifestyle_tips: Wash your face twice a day with a gentle cleanser
Avoid touching or picking at pimples
Manage stress levels as it can trigger flare-ups

description: An autoimmune disease that causes skin cells to build up and form scales and itchy, dry patches.
symptoms: Red patches of skin covered with thick, silvery scales
Dry, cracked skin that may bleed
medical_solutions: Salicylic Acid to promote sloughing of dead skin
lifestyle_tips: Take daily baths with salts or oils
Get limited amounts of sunlight (with caution)
Avoid triggers like smoking and heavy alcohol consumption

description: A condition that causes redness and often small, red, pus-filled bumps on the face.
lifestyle_tips: Identify and avoid triggers (spicy food, alcohol, extreme temperatures)
Use gentle, non-abrasive skin care products
Protect your face from wind and cold

description: A condition that causes brown or grayish patches on the skin, typically on the face.
symptoms: Dark patches on forehead, cheeks, or upper lip
natural_remedies: Aloe vera to help fade hyperpigmentation
lifestyle_tips: Use tinted sunscreen with iron oxides for extra protection

description: A skin infection caused by fungus.
lifestyle_tips: Keep the affected area clean and dry
Do not share personal items (towels, combs)
Wear breathable cotton socks and shoes

description: A red, itchy rash caused by direct contact with a substance or an allergic reaction to it.
symptoms: Bumps and blisters, sometimes with oozing and crusting
lifestyle_tips: Identify and avoid the substance that caused the reaction
Apply anti-itch cream or calamine lotion
Wash skin immediately after contact with a known trigger

description: Red, itchy welts that result from a skin reaction.
symptoms: Batches of red or skin-colored welts (wheals)
Welts that vary in size and change shape
natural_remedies: Baking soda paste to soothe itching
lifestyle_tips: Avoid known triggers (certain foods, medications, stress)
Apply cool cloths to the affected area

description: A disease that causes loss of skin color in patches.
symptoms: Premature whitening or graying of hair
Loss of color in tissues inside the mouth and nose
lifestyle_tips: Protect skin from the sun (sunburn can worsen it)
Use concealers or skin dyes for cosmetic appearance

description: A common condition with many causes, often environmental.
symptoms: Skin that feels and looks rough
lifestyle_tips: Shorten shower time and use lukewarm water

description: A general term that describes a common skin irritation.
A fungal infection that develops on the top layer of the skin.
medical_solutions: Oral antifungal meds for severe cases

description: An itchy skin condition caused by a tiny burrowing mite called Sarcoptes scabiei.
symptoms: Thin, irregular burrow tracks made of tiny blisters
lifestyle_tips: Wash all clothes/bedding in hot water

description: Painful, pus-filled bumps that form under the skin when bacteria infect one or more hair follicles.
symptoms: Develops a yellow or white tip
medical_solutions: Incision and drainage by a doctor

description: Red, painful, itchy skin that's hot to the touch, caused by overexposure to UV radiation.
An immune system reaction to a normally harmless substance.
A highly contagious skin infection that mainly affects infants and children.
symptoms: Red sores that rupture and form honey-colored crusts

description: A common, potentially serious bacterial skin infection.
medical_solutions: Hospitalization for IV antibiotics in severe cases
lifestyle_tips: Manage underlying conditions like athlete's foot

description: The most serious type of skin cancer, develops in the cells that produce melanin.
symptoms: Development of a new pigmented or unusual-looking growth
ABCDE rules: Asymmetry, Border, Color, Diameter, Evolving

description: An autoimmune disorder that causes patchy hair loss.
symptoms: Round or oval patches of baldness on the scalp or body
lifestyle_tips: Protect bald areas from the sun

description: An inflammatory condition that can affect skin, hair, nails, and mucous membranes.
symptoms: Lacy white patches in the mouth

description: A chronic skin condition that causes small, painful lumps to form under the skin.
A type of skin cancer that begins in the basal cells.
symptoms: A flat, flesh-colored or brown scar-like lesion
A bleeding or scabbing sore that heals and returns

description: A common form of skin cancer that develops in the squamous cells.
symptoms: A flat sore with a scaly crust
A new sore or raised area on an old scar or ulcer

description: A common skin condition in which hair follicles become inflamed.
symptoms: Small red bumps or white-headed pimples around hair follicles

description: A common fungal infection that causes small, discolored patches of skin.
symptoms: Patches of skin discoloration, usually on the back, chest, and upper arms
Patches may be lighter or darker than the surrounding skin
natural_remedies: Selsun Blue shampoo as body wash

description: A fungal infection of the fingernails or toenails.
symptoms: Debris building up under the nail
lifestyle_tips: Keep hands and feet clean and dry
Don't go barefoot in public areas

description: A viral infection caused by the herpes simplex virus.
symptoms: Tingling or burning sensation before blisters appear
lifestyle_tips: Avoid sharing utensils or lip balm

description: A viral infection that causes a painful rash.
symptoms: A red rash that begins a few days after the pain
Fluid-filled blisters that break open and crust over

description: An inherited or acquired skin condition that occurs when the skin doesn't shed its dead skin cells.
symptoms: Scales colored white, dirty gray or brown
medical_solutions: Alpha hydroxy acids (Lactic acid, Glycolic acid)
lifestyle_tips: Apply moisturizer while skin is damp

description: A common, harmless skin condition that causes dry, rough patches and tiny bumps, usually on upper arms, thighs, cheeks or buttocks.
symptoms: Dry, rough skin in the area of the bumps
medical_solutions: Creams to remove dead skin cells (AHAs, Salicylic acid)
lifestyle_tips: Don't pick or scrub the bumps

description: A rough, scaly patch on the skin that develops from years of sun exposure.
symptoms: Rough, dry or scaly patch of skin
Flat to slightly raised patch or bump
Hard, wart-like surface in some cases
lifestyle_tips: Regular skin checks by a dermatologist

description: A viral skin infection that results in round, firm, painless bumps.
symptoms: Indentation or dot in the center
medical_solutions: Cantharidin (beetle juice) applied by a doctor
lifestyle_tips: Cover bumps with bandages to prevent spread

description: A common skin condition that causes a temporary rash of raised, red, scaly patches on the body.
symptoms: Herald patch (a large, single scaly patch)
Smaller patches on the back or chest (Christmas tree pattern)
lifestyle_tips: The rash usually clears on its own in 6-12 weeks

description: A chronic inflammatory skin condition that most often affects the genital and anal areas.
symptoms: Bruising or tearing of the skin
natural_remedies: None (requires medical treatment to prevent scarring)

description: A rare skin condition that causes large, fluid-filled blisters.
symptoms: Redness or hive-like rash around blisters
lifestyle_tips: Avoid sun exposure to affected areas
Keep blisters clean to avoid infection

description: A rare, serious autoimmune disease that causes painful blisters on the skin and mucous membranes.
symptoms: Painful blisters that start in the mouth or on the skin
Blisters that rupture easily, leaving raw sores
lifestyle_tips: Avoid spicy or acidic foods if mouth is affected

description: A chronic, intensely itchy skin rash made up of bumps and blisters, often associated with celiac disease.
natural_remedies: Gluten-free diet is the primary natural management

description: A skin reaction that can be triggered by an infection or another medicine.
symptoms: Sores in the mouth (in severe cases)
lifestyle_tips: Identify the trigger (e.g., a specific medication)

description: Rares, serious disorders of the skin and mucous membranes, usually triggered by a medication.
symptoms: Painful red or purplish rash that spreads
Blisters on skin and mucous membranes
lifestyle_tips: Never take the triggering medication again

description: A chronic skin condition that consists of raised, reddish or skin-colored bumps that form a ring pattern.
symptoms: Ring pattern, usually on hands or feet
lifestyle_tips: Most cases resolve on their own without treatment

description: A rare, inflammatory skin disorder that causes large, painful sores to develop on the skin.
symptoms: Small red bump that quickly turns into a large, painful ulcer
Ulcer has a purple or blue border

description: A skin condition characterized by dark, velvety patches in body folds and creases.
symptoms: Dark, thick, velvety skin in armpits, groin or neck
Itching or odor in the affected area
medical_solutions: Treating the underlying cause (e.g., weight loss, diabetes management)
natural_remedies: Weight management through diet and exercise

description: A rare inherited condition in which there is an extreme sensitivity to ultraviolet (UV) rays from sunlight.
symptoms: Severe sunburn after minimal sun exposure
Development of many freckles at an early age

description: A group of rare diseases that cause fragile, blistering skin.
symptoms: Blistering of the skin from minor injury or friction

description: A chronic skin condition of sores with inflammation and scarring favoring the face, ears, and scalp.
A group of rare diseases that involve the hardening and tightening of the skin and connective tissues.
symptoms: Movement may be restricted if over a joint

description: An uncommon inflammatory disease marked by muscle weakness and a distinctive skin rash.
symptoms: Violet-colored or dusky red rash on face, eyelids, knuckles, elbows
lifestyle_tips: Sun protection (as it can trigger the rash)

description: Bright red, circular or oval skin growths caused by broken blood vessels.
natural_remedies: None (benign, but requires professional removal if desired)
lifestyle_tips: Usually harmless and doesn't require treatment

description: Common benign (non-cancerous) fibrous skin growths.
lifestyle_tips: Avoid irritating the growth by shaving over it

description: A slow-growing, fatty lump that's most often situated between your skin and the underlying muscle layer.
symptoms: Moves easily with slight finger pressure
lifestyle_tips: See a doctor if it becomes painful or grows rapidly

description: Small, dome-shaped bumps with a white or yellow appearance.
A bacterial skin infection involving the upper dermis and superficial lymphatics.
symptoms: Shiny, red, raised, and warm patch on skin
lifestyle_tips: Treat conditions like athlete's foot to prevent entry points

description: A fungal infection that usually begins between the toes.
medical_solutions: Oral antifungal meds for severe cases
lifestyle_tips: Don't go barefoot in public pools/gyms

description: A fungal infection in the skin of the genitals, inner thighs, and buttocks.
lifestyle_tips: Dry the groin area thoroughly after bathing

description: Infestation by tiny wingless insects that live on human hair and skin.
symptoms: Tickling feeling on scalp or body hair
lifestyle_tips: Seal non-washable items in plastic bags for 2 weeks

description: A skin condition in which itchy, coin-shaped spots or patches appear on the skin.
Skin inflammation that develops in people with poor circulation.
lifestyle_tips: Elevate legs above heart level frequently
Take short walks to improve circulation

description: An inflammatory rash that occurs around the mouth.
symptoms: Small, red, itchy bumps around the mouth, nose or eyes
Skin may be scaly or dry
lifestyle_tips: Avoid fluoride toothpaste for a while

description: Raised scars that grow much larger than the original injury.
symptoms: Grows beyond the site of the original injury
lifestyle_tips: Avoid unnecessary piercings or surgery if prone to keloids

description: Raised scars that remain within the boundary of the original injury.
symptoms: Does NOT grow beyond the original wound
lifestyle_tips: Keep the scar protected from the sun
Massage the scar as it heals
